title: move input_ids to hpu and remove disposal of adapter_meta
body:
move input_ids to hpu and remove disposal of adapter_meta

## What does this PR do?

Move input tensors (input_ids) onto HPU and remove/disallow disposal of adapter metadata (adapter_meta) so adapter metadata/module maps remain available (and not prematurely freed) after loading. Ensure generation code constructs small control tensors on the model device and that adapter-loading code does not free adapter metadata objects that callers expect to persist (e.g., to build BatchAdapterWeights or to reuse cached adapter configs).

(Description provided by submitter.)

step_plan.summary: Move input token tensors to the model device (HPU for Gaudi backend) early in batch construction and ensure small control tensors are created on the same device; remove any explicit disposal/clearing of adapter metadata in adapter-loading and adapter-preparation code so adapter metadata/module_map objects remain available for caching and downstream batch construction.

steps.operation: Stop disposing adapter metadata and ensure returned objects are intact
steps.reason: This function constructs adapter_config/module_map (adapter_meta). Remove any explicit deletion/clearing (del, .clear(), reassign to None, or .cpu() that invalidates references) so callers and the LRU cache retain usable adapter metadata for subsequent mapping and batch-preparation steps.
steps.side_effects: Increases peak memory usage slightly since metadata objects remain alive; tests that previously relied on freeing these objects will need to be updated. Ensures callers (e.g., BatchAdapterWeights construction) can access adapter metadata. No API signature change, but caching semantics are preserved; add tests to validate adapter reuse and memory.

steps.operation: Prevent disposal of input adapter metadata during merge orchestration
steps.reason: _load_and_merge creates/merges adapter metadata and module maps; ensure it does not delete or otherwise invalidate the original adapter_meta/module_map or adapter_config before returning the merged results so callers can rely on persistent metadata and caching.
steps.side_effects: Merged metadata remains in memory and in caches; potential increased memory usage. Avoids subtle crashes where callers expect metadata after merge. Tests for repeated load_and_merge and cached access should be run.

steps.operation: Avoid disposing adapter metadata during Lora per-adapter weight preparation
steps.reason: prepare_weights builds runtime tensors from adapter metadata; do not delete the adapter metadata/module_map after creating runtime tensors. Keep adapter metadata accessible for callers that will construct BatchLoraWeights or reuse cached configs.
steps.side_effects: Adapter metadata remains reachable (increased memory footprint). Ensures BatchLoraWeights.load and other downstream users can access metadata. Validate Gaudi-specific adapter conversion tests and ensure no double-free or duplicate-copy issues.

steps.operation: Keep adapter metadata available when constructing batch-level Lora weights
steps.reason: BatchLoraWeights.load uses adapter metadata to build per-batch pointer arrays; removing any explicit deletion of adapter metadata here prevents premature invalidation of objects callers may reuse (e.g., for caching or repeated batch construction).
steps.side_effects: Maintains references to adapter metadata (more memory retained). Ensures runtime kernels that rely on adapter metadata pointers have valid sources. Tests should confirm correct BatchLoraWeights construction and absence of stale pointers.

steps.operation: Avoid clearing adapter metadata while collecting per-layer adapter data
steps.reason: This function aggregates AdapterWeights and uses adapter metadata to build batch structures. Remove any in-function clearing/disposal of adapter metadata so BatchAdapterWeights and caller code can access the metadata post-call.
steps.side_effects: Caller code that expects adapter metadata to be available will work; memory retained may be larger. Unit tests for AdapterBatchData.from_meta and end-to-end adapter application should be run to detect regressions.

steps.operation: Make KVCache.store device-aware and validate/move keys/values to cache device or fail with clear error
steps.reason: With inputs moved to HPU, keys/values produced by projections may be on HPU and must match the cache device. Ensure KVCache.store either moves tensors to self.device explicitly (for small control-safe moves) or validates devices and raises a clear error so callers (prefill/forward) will produce compatible tensors.
steps.side_effects: May introduce implicit device moves if implemented to auto-move (affects performance); if strict validation is enforced, callers must ensure correct device placement, exposing earlier bugs. Update tests for KV cache prefill/passthrough and measure any performance change on Gaudi.

steps.operation: Move input tensors to model device in batch creation and compute position tensors on-device
steps.reason: Ensure input_ids, attention_mask, and any derived position_ids are moved to the model device early to avoid implicit host-device copies and runtime errors. This is the canonical spot for placing token tensors on the model device for non-Gaudi backends.
steps.side_effects: Prevents implicit device copies in downstream code; tests for generation must ensure inputs are correctly moved. Minor behaviour change: callers should not expect device-agnostic in-place ops on CPU inputs. Add/adjust tests that construct CausalLMBatch to reflect device placement.

steps.operation: Ensure Gaudi HPU backend moves inputs to HPU in batch construction
steps.reason: Specifically for the HPU/Gaudi backend, guarantee input_ids and attention_mask are .to(self.device) (HPU) and compute position_ids and other small control tensors on-device to avoid implicit device transfers and HPU kernel errors.
steps.side_effects: Prevents device-mismatch runtime errors on Gaudi; increases clarity of where tensors live. Must run Gaudi generation tests and KV cache integration tests to ensure no device mismatch remains.

steps.operation: Create small indexing/control tensors on the model device in generate_token (non-Gaudi)
steps.reason: generate_token creates small control tensors (token_idx, index arrays); ensure these are created with device=self.device to avoid implicit host-device copies during index_copy/index_select and to match input tensors moved earlier.
steps.side_effects: Small tensors created on-device avoid hidden copies and potential errors; may require update of tests that inspect CPU tensors. Performance will improve by removing implicit copies; validate generate tests and ensure correct dtype/device creation.

steps.operation: Create control tensors on HPU in generate_token and ensure device-consistent ops (Gaudi)
steps.reason: For HPU backend, ensure token_idx and any small indexing tensors are created directly on self.device (HPU) using torch.tensor(..., device=self.device) or x.new_tensor(...). This avoids implicit host<->HPU transfers and in-place errors in HPU kernels.
steps.side_effects: Removes hidden runtime transfers for Gaudi; may require small changes in tests that expect CPU-created tensors. Confirm end-to-end generation on Gaudi for correctness and performance.

steps.operation: Add guard/assumption and avoid implicit large tensor device moves in Gaudi forward
steps.reason: Forward should assume inputs are already on HPU (moved in from_pb). Add lightweight device assertions/early checks (raise informative error if input tensors are on wrong device) rather than implicitly moving large tensors inside forward, avoiding accidental host-device copies and performance regressions.
steps.side_effects: Breaks code that relied on forward implicitly moving tensors to HPU — callers must prepare inputs on device. Improves correctness and performance on Gaudi. Update integration tests and document the forward device precondition.
